Inspect the loaded nectar.toml config

Usage:
  nectar config [--config <path>] [--json] [--format <json|toml>] <subcommand...>

Arguments:
  subcommand          `print` (dump the fully resolved config after defaults, env overrides, and config layers), `get <dotted.key>` (print one value, e.g. `site.url` or `build.base_path`), or `path` (print the absolute path of the loaded config file, or nothing in plain mode / `null` in --json mode when no config was found)

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  -j, --json          Emit the value as JSON. For `print`: equivalent to `--format json`. For `get`: pretty-printed JSON of the value at the dotted path. For `path`: a `{ "config_path": "..." }` envelope so CI consumers can branch on `null` for "no config".
  --format <json|toml>  For `print`, choose the resolved config output format: `toml` (default) or `json`.
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → NECTAR_CONFIG_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  nectar config print                          # resolved config as TOML
  nectar config print --format json            # resolved config as JSON
  nectar config path                           # absolute path of the loaded toml
  nectar config get site.url
  nectar config get build.base_path --json
